An order line item breaks an order into an individual product purchase. From the business definition, it contains a unique line identifier, a reference to exactly one product, the quantity ordered, the unit price at the time of ordering, and the extended amount calculated as quantity Г— unit price.

The unit price is historical and fixed at order time, so it does not change if the product price changes later.
